responses.description: Menstrual cramps are common and are often caused by uterine contractions during the [PHASE] phase.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if pain is severe, sudden, or interferes with daily activities.

responses.description: Feeling tired during the [PHASE] phase is common due to hormonal fluctuations and changing energy demands.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if fatigue is persistent or severe.

responses.description: Bloating is often caused by hormonal changes and temporary water retention during the [PHASE] phase.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if bloating becomes painful or unusually severe.

responses.description: Hormonal changes during the [PHASE] phase can increase oil production and contribute to breakouts.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a dermatologist if acne becomes severe or persistent.

responses.description: Food cravings are common during the [PHASE] phase due to hormonal and metabolic changes.
Hormonal headaches can occur during the [PHASE] phase as estrogen levels fluctuate.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek immediate medical care for sudden severe headaches or neurological symptoms.

responses.description: Nausea may occur during the [PHASE] phase due to hormonal changes affecting digestion.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if nausea is severe or persistent.

responses.description: Feeling positive and energetic during the [PHASE] phase is a wonderful sign that your body and mind are in sync.
tips: Use this energy for important tasks

responses.description: Hormonal fluctuations during the [PHASE] phase can affect mood and emotional sensitivity.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek professional support if sadness becomes persistent or overwhelming.

responses.description: Hormonal changes can sometimes increase feelings of anxiety during the [PHASE] phase.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if anxiety significantly impacts daily life.

responses.description: High stress levels can influence hormones and even affect your cycle timing.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek support if stress feels difficult to manage for long periods.

responses.description: Rapid mood changes can occur during the [PHASE] phase due to hormone fluctuations.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if mood changes become severe or disruptive.

responses.description: PMS symptoms commonly appear during the luteal phase as hormone levels change before menstruation.
responses.doctorWarning: Speak with a healthcare professional if PMS symptoms become severe.

responses.description: Ovulation occurs when an ovary releases an egg. This is typically the most fertile time of your cycle.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you suspect ovulation issues or very irregular cycles.

responses.description: Fertility is generally highest around ovulation when an egg is available for fertilization.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if you've been trying to conceive for an extended period without success.

responses.description: Some early pregnancy symptoms can resemble PMS symptoms, including fatigue, breast tenderness, and nausea.
tips: Take a pregnancy test if your period is late
Consult a healthcare professional when needed
responses.doctorWarning: If you think you may be pregnant, consider confirming with a test and seeking medical guidance.

responses.description: A delayed period can be caused by stress, illness, travel, weight changes, or pregnancy.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if periods remain irregular or absent.

responses.description: Light spotting can occur at various times during the cycle and is often harmless.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if spotting becomes heavy, painful, or frequent.

responses.description: Vaginal discharge naturally changes throughout the menstrual cycle and can indicate fertility patterns.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if discharge develops an unusual odor, color, or causes discomfort.

responses.description: Breast tenderness is common during hormonal shifts, especially before menstruation.
tips: Apply a warm compress if needed
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if breast pain is severe, persistent, or associated with unusual changes.

responses.description: Sex drive naturally changes throughout the cycle and often increases around ovulation.
Nutrition needs can vary throughout the [PHASE] phase. Eating balanced meals helps support hormone health and energy levels.
Proper hydration helps support energy, digestion, skin health, and overall wellness during the [PHASE] phase.
tips: Aim for 8-10 glasses of water daily
Drink water regularly throughout the day
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if dehydration symptoms become severe.

responses.description: Hormonal changes during the [PHASE] phase can affect sleep quality and energy levels.
tips: Avoid caffeine late in the day
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if sleep problems persist.

responses.description: Exercise can improve mood, circulation, and energy throughout the [PHASE] phase.
responses.doctorWarning: Stop exercising and seek medical attention if you experience severe pain or dizziness.

responses.description: Iron supports healthy red blood cells and energy production, especially during menstruation.
tips: Pair iron-rich foods with vitamin C
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you suspect iron deficiency.

responses.description: Caffeine affects everyone differently and may influence sleep, anxiety, or certain cycle symptoms.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if caffeine causes significant symptoms.

responses.description: Alcohol can affect hydration, sleep quality, and overall wellness during the [PHASE] phase.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical help if alcohol use becomes difficult to control.

responses.description: Certain vitamins and minerals may help support overall wellness and menstrual health.
tips: Discuss supplements with a healthcare professional
Focus on food first when possible
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional before starting new supplements.

responses.description: Feeling tired during the [PHASE] phase is common and may be influenced by hormones, sleep, hydration, and nutrition.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if fatigue is severe or persistent.

responses.description: Nausea can occur during the [PHASE] phase due to hormonal fluctuations and prostaglandin activity.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if nausea is severe, persistent, or prevents eating and drinking.

responses.description: Hormonal changes during the [PHASE] phase can slow digestion and contribute to constipation.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if constipation becomes severe or prolonged.

responses.description: Some people experience diarrhea during menstruation because prostaglandins can affect the digestive system.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if diarrhea is severe, prolonged, or causes dehydration.

responses.description: Digestive discomfort can occur during the [PHASE] phase due to hormonal and gastrointestinal changes.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical attention if pain becomes severe or unusual.

responses.description: Hormonal fluctuations during the [PHASE] phase can contribute to bloating and increased gas.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if symptoms become severe or persistent.

responses.description: Digestive changes during the [PHASE] phase can sometimes increase indigestion or acid reflux symptoms.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if symptoms occur frequently or worsen.

responses.description: Hormonal changes, nausea, or stress during the [PHASE] phase may temporarily reduce appetite.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if appetite loss is significant or long-lasting.

responses.description: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome) is a hormonal condition that can affect periods, ovulation, weight, skin, and fertility.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional for diagnosis and personalized treatment.

responses.description: PCOD is a condition where the ovaries may develop multiple immature follicles, affecting hormone balance and menstrual cycles.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical guidance for proper evaluation and management.

responses.description: Endometriosis occurs when tissue similar to the uterine lining grows outside the uterus and may cause significant pain.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you experience severe menstrual pain or ongoing pelvic pain.

responses.description: Hormonal imbalances can affect mood, energy, skin, weight, and menstrual cycle regularity.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if symptoms become persistent or significantly affect daily life.

responses.description: Irregular periods can be caused by stress, hormonal changes, PCOS, thyroid issues, or other health conditions.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if cycles remain consistently irregular.

responses.description: Heavy menstrual bleeding may occur for many reasons, including hormonal changes and certain medical conditions.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical attention if bleeding is extremely heavy, prolonged, or causes weakness or dizziness.

responses.description: Pelvic pain can occur during different phases of the cycle and may have several possible causes.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical evaluation if pain is severe, persistent, or worsening.

responses.description: Hello! I'm Luna, your personal cycle and wellness companion. I'm here to help you understand your body and feel your best.
I'm Luna, your AI wellness companion. I can help explain symptoms, cycle phases, nutrition, hydration, sleep, and general menstrual wellness.
I'm doing great and ready to help. My favorite thing is helping you better understand your body and cycle.
I can answer questions about periods, symptoms, PMS, ovulation, fertility, nutrition, hydration, exercise, sleep, and emotional wellness.
Your privacy matters. Your health information should remain personal and secure within the app.
You're very welcome. I'm always here whenever you need guidance or support.
Healthcare professionals are the best source for diagnosis and treatment of medical conditions.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek urgent medical care for severe pain, heavy bleeding, fainting, difficulty breathing, or other emergency symptoms.

responses.description: Some symptoms require immediate medical attention and should not be managed through self-care alone.
tips: Do not delay treatment for severe symptoms
responses.doctorWarning: Get immediate medical care if you experience severe pain, loss of consciousness, heavy uncontrolled bleeding, or other emergency symptoms.

responses.description: The Menstrual Phase is the beginning of your cycle when the uterine lining sheds. Energy levels may be lower and cramps, fatigue, and mood changes are common.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if bleeding is extremely heavy or pain is severe.

responses.description: The Follicular Phase begins after menstruation. Estrogen rises, energy often improves, and many people feel more motivated and productive.
Ovulation is when an egg is released from the ovary. Fertility is highest during this phase and energy may peak.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you suspect ovulation-related problems.

responses.description: The Luteal Phase occurs after ovulation and before menstruation. PMS symptoms, cravings, bloating, and mood changes are more common.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if PMS symptoms become severe.

responses.description: Energy levels naturally change throughout the menstrual cycle due to hormone fluctuations.
Hormonal fluctuations throughout the cycle can influence mood, motivation, and emotional sensitivity.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek professional support if emotional symptoms become overwhelming.

responses.description: I can review your hydration data and provide personalized guidance.
Travel, time-zone changes, and disrupted routines can temporarily affect your cycle.
Hormonal changes throughout the cycle can affect focus, memory, and productivity.
tips: Prioritize important tasks during high-energy days

responses.description: Self-care can help improve both physical and emotional wellbeing during every phase of your cycle.
Motivation naturally changes throughout the menstrual cycle as hormone levels fluctuate.
Hormones can influence skin appearance, oil production, and overall glow throughout the cycle.
Hormonal fluctuations can affect hair texture, oil production, and shedding patterns.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if hair loss becomes significant.

responses.description: Pads are external menstrual products that absorb menstrual flow.
Tampons are internal menstrual products used to absorb menstrual flow.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical attention if you experience symptoms of Toxic Shock Syndrome.

responses.description: Menstrual cups are reusable products designed to collect menstrual flow.
Birth control methods help prevent pregnancy and may also affect menstrual cycles, symptoms, and hormone levels.
tips: Discuss options with a healthcare professional
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional when choosing or changing birth control methods.

responses.description: Birth control pills contain hormones that help prevent pregnancy and may regulate periods.
tips: Take pills at the same time daily
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if you experience severe side effects or concerns.

responses.description: Missing a birth control pill may reduce effectiveness depending on the type of pill and timing.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you're unsure what to do after missing pills.

responses.description: Condoms help reduce the risk of pregnancy and sexually transmitted infections when used correctly.
tips: Use a new condom each time

responses.description: An IUD is a long-term contraceptive device placed in the uterus by a healthcare professional.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical care if you experience severe pain, fever, or unusual bleeding.

responses.description: A contraceptive implant is a small device placed under the skin that releases hormones to prevent pregnancy.
tips: Discuss concerns with your healthcare provider
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you notice significant side effects.

responses.description: Emergency contraception can help reduce the chance of pregnancy after unprotected intercourse when used within the recommended timeframe.
tips: Take it as soon as possible
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if your period is significantly delayed or you have concerns.

responses.description: Light spotting or breakthrough bleeding can occur when starting or changing hormonal contraception.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if bleeding becomes heavy or persistent.

responses.description: Trying to conceive involves understanding ovulation, fertile windows, and maintaining healthy lifestyle habits.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you've been trying to conceive for an extended period without success.

responses.description: The fertile window is the period when pregnancy is most likely, typically around ovulation.
Common ovulation signs include stretchy cervical mucus, increased libido, mild pelvic discomfort, and slight temperature changes.
Early pregnancy symptoms may include a missed period, fatigue, nausea, breast tenderness, and increased urination.
tips: Take a pregnancy test if appropriate
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional for confirmation and prenatal care.

responses.description: Pregnancy tests are generally most accurate after a missed period.
tips: Use first morning urine if recommended
Consult a healthcare professional for confirmation

responses.description: Implantation occurs when a fertilized egg attaches to the uterine lining. Some people may notice light spotting.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if you experience concerning symptoms.

responses.description: Nausea and vomiting are common during early pregnancy and can occur at any time of day.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical attention if nausea becomes severe or causes dehydration.

responses.description: Good nutrition supports both maternal health and fetal development during pregnancy.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dietary guidance.

responses.description: Your first period is a normal part of puberty and signals that your reproductive system is maturing.
Puberty causes physical and emotional changes as hormones increase and the body develops.
Having your period at school can feel stressful, but it's a common experience.
tips: Talk to a teacher or nurse if needed

responses.description: Yes, you can swim during your period. Many people continue normal activities while menstruating.
Exercise during your period is generally safe and may help improve mood and reduce discomfort.
Washing your hair during your period is completely safe and does not affect menstrual flow.
tips: Use products that suit your hair type

responses.description: Pregnancy is less likely during menstruation, but it is still possible depending on cycle timing.
tips: Use reliable contraception if avoiding pregnancy

responses.description: Perimenopause is the transition period before menopause when hormone levels begin to fluctuate.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional if symptoms become difficult to manage.

responses.description: Menopause occurs when menstrual periods permanently stop due to natural hormonal changes.
responses.doctorWarning: Consult a healthcare professional regarding persistent symptoms.

responses.description: Hot flashes are sudden feelings of warmth that commonly occur during menopause.
Night sweats are episodes of excessive sweating during sleep and can occur during menopause.
A Pap smear is a screening test used to detect abnormal cervical cells.
tips: Discuss questions with your healthcare provider

responses.description: Breast self-awareness helps you notice changes that should be discussed with a healthcare professional.
tips: Become familiar with your normal breast appearance.
responses.doctorWarning: Seek medical advice if you notice new lumps or concerning changes.

responses.description: A gynecologist specializes in reproductive and women's health.
You slept [SLEEP] hours recently.
